schools.summary: Gill: 'these are the words of the covenant' looks to what follows, full of promises of grace to Jew and Gentile.
Keil & Delitzsch: the addresses renewing the covenant in Moab.

themes: These are the words of the covenant... in the land of Moab, beside the covenant... in Horeb
the covenant renewed in a new generation
the bond with God entered afresh (Jer. 31:31)

schools.summary: Gill: he summoned the whole body of the people, a solemn covenant being to be made; they had seen all the LORD did in Egypt.

themes: Ye have seen all that the LORD did before your eyes in the land of Egypt
the covenant grounded in remembered mercy
the witnessed works the spur to obey (Rom. 12:1)

schools.summary: Gill: the ten plagues which tried the Egyptians and tried Israel whether they would believe and trust God's power.
Keil & Delitzsch: the great temptations, signs, and miracles.

themes: The great temptations which thine eyes have seen, the signs, and those great miracles
God richly attesting his power
abundant ground for faith, witnessed wonders (Ps. 78:11)

schools.summary: Gill: they had seen the miracles with their bodily eyes, but had not discerned with the eyes of their understanding the power and goodness of God in them.

themes: the LORD hath not given you an heart to perceive, and eyes to see, and ears to hear
man's need of grace even to understand
none knows without a heart from above (1 Cor. 2:14)

schools.summary: Gill: from their coming out of Egypt to that day, a round number of forty years; their clothes and shoes did not wear out.

themes: your clothes are not waxen old upon you, and thy shoe is not waxen old upon thy foot
a standing miracle of God's care
his people lacking nothing through the wilderness (Deut. 2:7)

schools.summary: Gill: they ate not bread of their own making, but manna, the food of angels, the bread of heaven.
Keil & Delitzsch: that ye might know that I am the LORD your God.

themes: Ye have not eaten bread... that ye might know that I am the LORD your God
the daily manna teaching dependence on God
the bread of heaven pointing to Christ (John 6:32)

schools.summary: Gill: their coming to the borders of Moab. Calvin: a third instance of God's goodness, in smiting the first enemies who impeded their passage, the conquest already begun.

themes: Sihon the king of Heshbon, and Og the king of Bashan, came out against us... and we smote them
the latest token of God's goodness
God's help hitherto and onward (1 Sam. 7:12)

schools.summary: Gill: the lands of Jazer, Gilead, and Bashan, fine countries for pasturage, given to Reuben, Gad, and half Manasseh.

themes: we took their land, and gave it for an inheritance unto the Reubenites, and to the Gadites, and to the half tribe of Manasseh
the first earnest of the inheritance
the part assuring the whole (2 Cor. 1:22)

schools.summary: Gill: laid under great obligations by God's goodness — the victory over the two kings and all the favours in the wilderness; keeping the covenant the way to prosper.

themes: Keep therefore the words of this covenant, and do them, that ye may prosper in all that ye do
obedience the grateful response to grace
the keeping of the covenant the path of blessing (Josh. 1:8)

schools.summary: Gill: gathered at the door of the tabernacle; Aben Ezra, round about the ark, the symbol of the divine Presence.
Keil & Delitzsch: all of them standing before the LORD.

themes: Ye stand this day all of you before the LORD your God; your captains... your elders... with all the men of Israel
the whole nation embraced, none excepted
all alike his in the covenant (Gal. 3:28)

schools.summary: Gill: the little ones and wives, scarce ever mentioned in any special law or solemn transaction.
Keil & Delitzsch: the covenant embraced even the stranger and the meanest servants.

themes: Your little ones, your wives, and thy stranger... from the hewer of thy wood unto the drawer of thy water
the covenant reaching to the lowest
grace despising none, the promise to all (Acts 2:39)

schools.summary: Gill: they were to appear and stand to solemnly avouch him their God and hear him declare them his people, the covenant and oath made with them that day.

themes: That thou shouldest enter into covenant with the LORD thy God, and into his oath
a solemn, sworn engagement on both sides
the promise confirmed by God's oath (Heb. 6:18)

schools.summary: Gill: this contains the sum and substance of the covenant.
Keil & Delitzsch: establishing them as a people unto himself, as sworn to the fathers.

themes: That he may establish thee... for a people unto himself, and that he may be unto thee a God
the mutual belonging, the heart of the covenant
I will be their God, and they my people (Jer. 31:33)

schools.summary: Gill: Moses was ordered to make this covenant in the name of the Lord, delivering his promises and requirements; it was not with them only.

themes: Neither with you only do I make this covenant and this oath
the covenant not bound to one generation
the promise to your children afar off (Acts 2:39)

schools.summary: Gill: those present are specified by dignity, age, sex, and station; the others are those not yet in being.
Keil & Delitzsch: the covenant with him that is not here this day.

themes: with him that standeth here with us this day... and also with him that is not here with us this day
the covenant reaching the unborn generations
his mercy unto children's children (Ps. 103:17)

schools.summary: Gill: they and their fathers had dwelt in Egypt upwards of two hundred years, a land much given to idolatry, and passed through other nations.

themes: ye know how we have dwelt in the land of Egypt; and how we came through the nations
exposed to the contagion of idolatry
called to come out and be separate (2 Cor. 6:17)

schools.summary: Gill: the idols are called 'abominations,' abominable to God and to be so to men; of wood and stone, silver and gold.

themes: ye have seen their abominations, and their idols, wood and stone, silver and gold
the vanity and folly of idolatry
idols the work of men's hands (Ps. 115:4)

schools.summary: Gill: these words show the design of the solemn covenant — to prevent their falling into idolatry; such a one is a root bearing bitter herbs.

themes: whose heart turneth away this day from the LORD our God... a root that beareth gall and wormwood
the danger of secret apostasy
lest a root of bitterness defile many (Heb. 12:15)

schools.summary: Gill: the man before compared to a root of bitter herbs, hearing the curses pronounced against such as himself.
Keil & Delitzsch: him blessing himself in his heart.

themes: he bless himself in his heart, saying, I shall have peace, though I walk in the imagination of mine heart
the deadly self-deception of false peace
crying peace where there is no peace (Jer. 6:14)

schools.summary: Gill: the Lord will have no mercy on him, being a hardhearted, impenitent, obstinate sinner.
Ellicott: the anger that 'shall smoke,' comparing the Psalms.

themes: The LORD will not spare him, but then the anger of the LORD and his jealousy shall smoke against that man
presumption inviting the heaviest judgment
the hardened suddenly destroyed (Prov. 29:1)

schools.summary: Gill: the Lord devotes and consigns him to the evil of punishment, a visible mark of his displeasure, according to all the curses written in the book.

themes: the LORD shall separate him unto evil out of all the tribes of Israel
the apostate singled out for the heavier doom
the false parted to judgment (Matt. 13:30)

schools.summary: Gill: not the next generation but future ages, even after the destruction of Judea by the Romans. Calvin: God enforces the certainty of the punishment.

themes: the generation to come... and the stranger that shall come from a far land, shall say, when they see the plagues
the desolation a wonder to later ages
an astonishment among all nations (Deut. 28:37)

schools.summary: Gill: the land brimstone and salt, not sown nor bearing.
Keil & Delitzsch: the likeness to the overthrow of Sodom, Gomorrah, Admah, and Zeboim.

themes: the whole land thereof is brimstone, and salt, and burning... like the overthrow of Sodom
the land made a monument of wrath
set forth for an example (Jude 7)

schools.summary: Gill: the destruction so great and awful that its fame would be heard among all nations, who would ask the question.

themes: all nations shall say, Wherefore hath the LORD done thus unto this land?
God's judgment preaching to the world
the LORD known by the judgment he executeth (Ps. 9:16)

schools.summary: Gill: this is the answer returned to the questions.
Keil & Delitzsch: because they forsook the covenant the LORD made when he brought them out of Egypt.

themes: Because they have forsaken the covenant of the LORD God of their fathers
the judgment for forsaking the covenant
they forgat God their saviour (Ps. 106:21)

schools.summary: Gill: done by all Israel in Solomon's time, the ten tribes under Jeroboam, and Judah under Ahaz and Manasseh, worshipping the host of heaven; gods whom they knew not.

themes: they went and served other gods, and worshipped them, gods whom they knew not
idolatry forsaking the known God for vanities
the fountain left for broken cisterns (Jer. 2:13)

schools.summary: Gill: the anger for their idolatry and base ingratitude.
Keil & Delitzsch: the anger kindled to bring all the curses of the book upon the land.

themes: the anger of the LORD was kindled against this land, to bring upon it all the curses that are written in this book
the threatened curses surely performed
the word shall judge the covenant-breaker (John 12:48)

schools.summary: Gill: true both at the Babylonish captivity and at their present dispersion by the Romans, by whom they have been rooted out these 1700 years.

themes: the LORD rooted them out of their land in anger, and in wrath... and cast them into another land
uprooting and exile for covenant-breaking
led captive into all nations (Luke 21:24)

schools.summary: Gill: the secret things respect God's providential dealings, especially their final rejection, like the apostle's exclamation at the depth of God's judgments; the revealed things belong to us and our children, that we may do the law.

themes: The secret things belong unto the LORD our God: but those things which are revealed belong unto us and to our children
submission to the hidden, obedience to the revealed
the things revealed enough for faith and duty (Rom. 11:33)
